span.p:
manifest.json
page table: roles · digests ·
real extractive descriptor · QUARANTINE state

div.style: color: rgb(58, 42, 102) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
color: rgb(122, 62, 18) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
live session
tool results stream in

div.style: color: rgb(27, 58, 102) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
ctxmmu gate pages each result to a
content-addressed store at WRITE time
(quarantine decided here, once)

div.style: color: rgb(110, 31, 27) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
PROCESS BOUNDARY
session ends = a core dump

div.style: color: rgb(27, 58, 102) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
recall.Load(dir)
rebuilds a Session with its OWN CAS + a FRESH ctxmmu gate
verifies every blob vs its digest → a tampered device FAILS CLOSED

div.style: color: rgb(28, 82, 48) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
new Session, self-contained,
ready to Resolve / Recall

div.style: color: rgb(70, 88, 106) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
the seal OUTLIVES the process: a page the gate quarantined
before the boundary stays sealed after it — no memory system
that re-pastes transcript bytes has this. recall makes the
decision DURABLE + re-screenable; it does not change the decision.
